title: Marine Le Pen decides to run for president after appeals convition
subhead: The far-right leader said she would appeal to France's highest court after her embezzlement conviction was upheld but her ban on holding elected office was shortened, allowing her to stand in the 2027 election.

title: US launches new strikes on Iran after Hormuz attacks
Pyrénées wildfires destroy everything in their path: 'I didn't think I'd make it out alive'
How Malian jihadists and pro-independence groups joined forces to overthrow the junta
subhead: For years, jihadists from JNIM and pro-independence fighters from the FLA harbored deadly hatred for each other. But they put their differences aside to confront their common enemy: the ruling junta in Bamako. Since April 25, they have launched a major offensive.

title: Farage to resign as MP, force by-election amid financial scrutiny
subhead: The Reform UK leader said he would seek re-election in Clacton, a move that suspends parliamentary investigations into undisclosed donations, including from a convicted fraudster.

title: French prosecutors open probe into Paraguay senator's racist abuse of Mbappé
subhead: Celeste Amarilla mocked Kylian Mbappé's origins, upbringing and appearance on X after his winning penalty knocked Paraguay out of the World Cup.

title: Book reveals Trump's improvisation and concentration of power
subhead: Two journalists from the 'New York Times' retrace the early months of the US president's second term, highlighting the ruptures and upheavals his approach to power has unleashed.

Vladimir Putin, or the loneliness of 'Homo Sovieticus'
subhead: Between denial of reality, a desire for revenge and carefully crafted messaging, the Russian president has tried to position himself as the master of the game. But after more than four years of war in Ukraine, these old tricks inherited from the Soviet past are showing their limitations, and he is losing credibility.

title: Netanyahu government's legal coup challenges Israel's justice system: 'The final nail in the coffin of democracy'
subhead: By declaring he would not comply with a Supreme Court ruling, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is directly confronting the country's highest legal authority, plunging Israel into a constitutional crisis.

title: Politicial scientist Alexandra de Hoop Scheffer: 'The United States is grappling with strategic saturation'
subhead: According to the president of the German Marshall Fund, the US, which is engaged on multiple fronts, is renegotiating the transatlantic contract unilaterally by pressuring its European allies to take responsibility for their own security. This 'burden shifting' challenges Europe to coordinate the organization of its defense industries.
